description: Lock Pilgrimage Route Services to medieval pilgrimage-route support infrastructure rather than merchant couriers or generic hospital wards.

checks.reason: UNESCO anchors the Santiago route evidence between the 11th and 15th centuries, so the node uses a conservative century-level medieval route-services date.
Road and route corridors are the physical substrate for pilgrimage route services.
Charitable hospitals and hospices are the institutional substrate for pilgrim shelter and care.
Merchant courier systems are commercial messaging infrastructure, not a direct prerequisite for pilgrim route services.
Generic patient wards are narrower medical organization than the charitable hospital and hospice network that supported pilgrims.
